objects.name: Command and Scripting Interpreter: PowerShell
objects.description: Adversaries may abuse PowerShell commands and scripts for execution. PowerShell is a powerful interactive command-line interface and scripting environment included in the Windows operating system. Adversaries can use PowerShell to perform a number of actions, including discovery of information and execution of code.
objects.x_mitre_detection: Monitor for PowerShell execution, including use of the -enc flag. Event ID 4104 (Script Block Logging) captures PowerShell script content. PowerShell 5.0+ transcript logging can also record commands and output.
x_mitre_mitigations: Disable or restrict PowerShell via AppLocker or Windows Defender Application Control (WDAC).
Use anti-malware solutions to detect malicious PowerShell activity.

objects.name: System Binary Proxy Execution
objects.description: Adversaries may bypass process and/or signature-based defenses by proxying execution of malicious content with signed, or otherwise trusted, binaries. Windows systems use a variety of signed binaries for normal operations.
objects.x_mitre_detection: Monitor for execution of system binaries from unusual parent processes or with unusual command-line arguments. Correlated process creation events (Sysmon Event ID 1, Windows Event ID 4688) can identify proxy execution.
x_mitre_mitigations: Restrict binary execution via AppLocker or WDAC.
Monitor and alert on proxy execution patterns.
